Hey, you must be
that's right, you're the private detective, Guy.
Yes,
my name is Snipes.
It's you. I thought, since you're a private detective, you'd be some kind of shady guy,
but surprisingly, you're actually a nice young man.
I'm hardly a good guy.
No, you're a good young man. I have an eye for character. If I'm saying it, it must be true. Hahaha.
Thank you very much.
So, how's the conversation flowing with everyone?

? I wonder who that was.
He’s my assistant.
Your assistant? Oh, that’s her talking to Patty. But, is it really okay to have such a young kid as an assistant?
She’s actually 20 years old. She just looks young because her mother is Japanese.
Wow, older than Patty, huh?
No way, that’s surprising.
I thought she was around 15.
Oh my.
You really can’t judge a woman's age by her looks.
Didn’t you have any doubts about her age when you first met her?
I had some background knowledge since I received her resume earlier.
Still, I wonder if she’s your type.
Asian girls do tend to look younger.
I want to ask you something.
Is her outreach work your doing?
Not really, but...
Ah, I thought so. Her audacity is commendable, but if she stands out too much, it makes some people uncomfortable.
Sorry, could you tell her to stop the outreach activities?
I apologize. I’ll make sure to talk to her about it.
Yeah. I’d appreciate it.
She’s not a bad kid.
She’s my only daughter. I lost her mother early on and left her with a housekeeper, but she’s grown up well.
She’s a cute girl.
Cute? That girl?!
She’s so logical about everything; it really undermines my authority. I wonder what she thinks of her father.
She’s of age but shows no interest in boys; not even a hint of a boyfriend.
You don't have to rush; you'll surely find a wonderful boyfriend someday.
I hope so.
It seems I'm not good at topics like this. When it comes to marrying her off, I might get a bit teary.
The reason that she turned into such an unlovable woman might be because she was raised only by her father.
Did Patty do something rude?
No, not particularly.
I see. Well, that's good.
She tends to act awkwardly around people she's liked for a long time.
That girl has few friends her age, and I worry about that as a parent.
But she’s a bright girl, so she must have lots of friends, right?
No, it seems not. Since she grew up among adults, she doesn't interact well with her peers.
I think she's probably more dependable than you might be thinking.
Is that so?
By the way, if she insists on wanting to be a detective, please don't take her seriously. I think it's just a passing whim.
Tonny Ford, huh?
I've known him for a long time. He’s not related to this movie, but I invited him anyway.
I see.
He may have some, well,
bad rumors swirling around, but at his core, he’s a good guy.
Tonny isn't good at socializing. If he made you feel uncomfortable, I apologize.
No, that’s not the case.
He’s a talented actor, but being great at one thing doesn’t mean he excels at everything.
He really is a nice guy, though.
If Tonny said something rude to you, please don’t take it personally. He has this self-deprecating way of enjoying being disliked.
Haven't you heard anything from Tony? Uh, it's not something pleasant.
Is it about Jennifer?
Ah, so he did say something. Everything he's saying is nonsense.
He holds a grudge against Jennifer.
It might have been a mistake to call him.
Is it Cindy's agent?
I don't know much about her.
It seems she originally wanted to be an actress.
An agent who wanted to be an actress, huh.
Well, it’s common, but there are plenty of people who want to be actresses.
Yeah. She's amazing in her own way, since she has brought a wonderful talent like Cindy into the world.
That's true. She's accomplished the great feat of producing and bringing Cindy to the world.
She takes good care of Cindy. You can tell she's really working hard from the outside.
Is that so?
Being an agent must be tough.
It’s really tough. She has to manage everything, not just Cindy's schedule.
It's like being a mother.
No, it feels more like she’s a wife, really.
I wish she could be a bit better at socializing.
She seems to hold a grudge against Guy.
That’s something you can’t just ignore.
Yeah. It looks like she still holds a grudge from when Guy hugged Cindy during their first meeting.
But can someone really hold a grudge over just that?
Uh.
Yeah, she’s special.
Earlier, she almost attacked Guy with a fork.
Did that really happen!?
Yeah.
At that rate, it wouldn't be surprising if she had done something in the past.
But it's better not to worry about her strange attitude.
Cindy is great. I think that girl could become an indispensable figure in the American film industry.
That's incredible.
Yeah, that girl has something that appeals to Americans.
I mean, she has that quality.
For some reason, that girl is trying way too hard to act cute. She could easily make it without doing that.
Isn't that the agent's policy?
Maybe, but they must know that isn't the way to highlight her.
I believe she can succeed as an actress.
I want to reveal her true self in this movie because I think it's for her best.
But doesn't that involve some risks?
I feel like they won't allow that.
However, I'm the director. If it's for Cindy's sake,
she should walk the path of an actress here.
Cindy is like a raw gem that is being polished the wrong way. She needs to be polished correctly to shine more.
I hope the film brings out a new side of her.
Not a new side, but her true self. I will bring her back to the way she should be.
Keep a close eye on her; watch her future.
She is in her prime now, and I intend for her to add color to the next film.
I'm sure it has something to do with madness.
You don't seem very knowledgeable. Normally, she has a neat vibe, but she's good at playing intense women in her roles.
Actually, she's the actress I'm currently most enthusiastic about. I think her acting is genuine.
Seems like she's quite your favorite.
Of course! She's an actress who will shine even more from now on.
I believe she's going to leap forward even more in the future.
Why did Jennifer incur Tonny's resentment?
I don't know the details, but I think it was probably due to some romantic entanglement.
So saying he resents her is
an immature act, isn't it?
But there are things that can't be so simply decided.
I plan to keep casting her more and more.
Her acting has a solid reputation. She will surely support the film well.
She's a great actress, isn't she?
Yeah, that's right.
I think she's working hard as a mid-level actress in the film industry.
Is the director going to marry Meryl?
No.
Where on earth did you hear that?
But
oh man, my daughter is a handful.
Let me clarify, Meryl and I are definitely not in a relationship that would lead to marriage.
Is that so?
Yeah. There are various rumors floating around, but that is not the case at all.
She is an excellent actress and I am a director.
There’s nothing else to it.
I think he’s a good character. He’s in his prime now.
In his prime now.
Exactly right. But I think he can't act.
No, no, that's not true. He's actually quite a performer.
Is that so?
Yeah, yeah. Even if he retires, he might still be able to make it as an actor.
You know, I really like the character named He.
Hearing that much must make Guy happy.
Is that so? He kind of looked like he was bothered, though.
He seems like someone destined to be popular.
Guy is great. The more I talk to him, the more I get drawn in.
He's always been a well-liked guy.
Yeah, he has charisma, and I think he could become an actor if he wanted to—as long as he has the acting skills to back it up.
If Guy didn't have a fiancée, I'd want him to marry my daughter and call him my son.
Yeah, she’s a beautiful person. But there’s something about her that feels a bit unapproachable.
Really?
You might not feel that way since you've known her since school,
but you know, there’s this aura that makes it hard to casually strike up a conversation.
Well, that might be true. She has always had this air of superiority towards people.
That’s true. Her gaze is intimidating.
I think she's pretty, but that atmosphere really cuts her charm in half.
I'll be sure to mention it to her.
Hey, come on, that’s not a joke. If that happens, she'd probably hold a grudge for life.
She’s not that terrible of a woman.
No, I think so too,
but I still find her kind of scary.
Don’t say anything reckless to her.
I was just a bit curious, but are Guy and her fighting?
Did something happen?
No, I just haven’t seen them talking together, so I'm a little worried.
Could it be that she doesn’t like me talking to Guy all the time?
I don't think that's it.
I hope that’s the case.
If you can't have fun despite the invitation, it means I'm not cut out to be a host.
Are you familiar with the serial murder case?
Is something going on again? I'm not really in the loop on those things.
Then you might know something.
Is that so?
You're really a detective, aren't you? Bringing up a gory incident at the party.
What's it to you?
No, it’s just that someone claimed to know the killer.
At least the people here can’t be the killer. That person who said they knew the killer was probably just joking.
It's important not to be swayed by bad rumors.
※※※ It's a bug ※※※
